children.heading: Appropriate conservation project
children.label: 1 Appropriate conservation project
children.text: The term “appropriate conservation project” means any project for the conservation, restoration, construction or rehabilitation of natural, cultural, historic, archaeological, recreational, or scenic resources.

children.heading: Corps and Public Lands Corps
children.label: 2 Corps and Public Lands Corps
children.text: The terms “Corps” and “Public Lands Corps” mean the Public Lands Corps established by .section 1723(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Eligible service lands
children.label: 3 Eligible service lands
children.text: The term “eligible service lands” means public lands, Indian lands, and Hawaiian home lands.

children.heading: Hawaiian home lands
children.label: 4 Hawaiian home lands
children.text: The term “Hawaiian home lands” means all lands given the status of Hawaiian home lands under section 204 of the Hawaiian Homes Commission Act, 1920 (), or under the corresponding provision of the Constitution of the State of Hawaii adopted under section 4 of the Act entitled “An Act to provide for the admission of the State of Hawaii into the Union”, approved (; ).42 Stat. 110Public Law 86–373 Stat. 5March 18, 1959

children.label: A
children.text: is a member of an Indian tribe; or

children.label: B
children.text: is a “Native”, as defined in section 3(b) of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ().43 U.S.C. 1602(b)

children.label: A
children.text: any Indian reservation;

children.label: B
children.text: any public domain Indian allotments;

children.label: C
children.text: any former Indian reservation in the State of Oklahoma;

children.label: D
children.text: any land held by incorporated Native groups, regional corporations, and village corporations under the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act [ et seq.]; and43 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: E
children.text: any land held by dependent Indian communities within the borders of the United States whether within the original or subsequently acquired territory thereof, and whether within or without the limits of a State.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 7 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” means an Indian tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community, including any Native village, Regional Corporation, or Village Corporation, as defined in subsection (c), (g), or (j), respectively, of section 3 of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (, (g), or (j)), that is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States under Federal law to Indians because of their status as Indians.43 U.S.C. 1602(c)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 1002 of title 20

children.label: i
children.text: an institution described in ; orsection 1001(b) of title 20

children.label: ii
children.text: an institution outside the United States, as described in .section 1002(a)(1)(C) of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: To reduce wildfire risk to a community, municipal water supply, or other at-risk Federal land.

children.label: B
children.text: To protect a watershed or address a threat to forest and rangeland health, including catastrophic wildfire.

children.label: C
children.text: To address the impact of insect or disease infestations or other damaging agents on forest and rangeland health.

children.label: i
children.text: promote the recovery of threatened or endangered species;

children.label: ii
children.text: improve biological diversity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: enhance productivity and carbon sequestration.

children.label: i
children.text: to promote the recovery of threatened species, endangered species, and managed fisheries;

children.label: ii
children.text: to restore fisheries, protected resources, and habitats impacted by oil and chemical spills and natural disasters; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to enhance the resilience of coastal ecosystems, communities, and economies through habitat conservation.

children.heading: Public lands
children.label: 10 Public lands
children.text: The term “public lands” means any lands or waters (or interest therein) owned or administered by the United States, except that such term does not include any Indian lands.

children.label: A
children.text: is capable of offering meaningful, full-time, productive work for individuals between the ages of 16 and 30, inclusive, or veterans age 35 or younger in a natural or cultural resource setting;

children.label: B
children.text: gives participants a mix of work experience, basic and life skills, education, training, and support services; and

children.label: C
children.text: provides participants with the opportunity to develop citizenship values and skills through service to their community and the United States.

children.heading: Resource assistant
children.label: 12 Resource assistant
children.text: The term “resource assistant” means a resource assistant selected under .section 1725 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to National Forest System land, the Secretary of Agriculture;

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to Indian lands, Hawaiian home lands, or land administered by the Department of the Interior, the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: C
children.text: with respect to the National Marine Sanctuary System, coral reefs, and other coastal, estuarine, and marine habitats, and other land and facilities administered by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, the Virgin Islands of the United States, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Veteran
children.label: 15 Veteran
children.text: The term “veteran” has the meaning given the term in .section 101 of title 38
